nodes.norm_label: note: the asg.1 / asg.2 consultant-memo link keeps its own dynamic label

nodes.label: NOTE: Program Directors do NOT require specialtyId per backend rules.
nodes.norm_label: note: program directors do not require specialtyid per backend rules.

nodes.label: Claude Codex Prompt Reviewer Skill
nodes.rationale: Improve a prompt before it is sent to Codex so Codex works safely, clearly, and with fewer mistakes; separate safe from risky changes and stop before commit unless approved.

nodes.label: Claude Codex Work Reviewer Skill
nodes.rationale: Review Codex's completed work (git diff, changed files, tests, security risk) before commit/merge/deploy, producing a SAFE TO COMMIT / NEEDS FIXES / DO NOT COMMIT verdict.

nodes.label: MTMS Agent Routing (Coordinator)
nodes.rationale: The Coworker acts as coordinator: understand, inspect, route each part to the correct agent/model, review/merge outputs, detect conflicts, and verify before declaring done, without weakening security or exposing secrets.

nodes.label: MTMS Security Audit and Honeypot Skill
nodes.rationale: Authorized defensive security testing only: improve MTMS security by checking auth, RBAC, API protection, spam abuse, uploads, and honeypots without destructive or DoS attacks.
nodes.norm_label: mtms security audit and honeypot skill

nodes.label: Model Routing Rules
nodes.rationale: Frontend/UI goes to Claude Code + Fable 5; backend/security/database goes to Codex + GPT-5.5; full-stack is split and both outputs compared against one API contract, splitting work by model strength.

nodes.label: Secret Protection Safety Rule
nodes.rationale: Never print/expose secrets from .env files; if found, report only the file path and state it must be rotated. Never commit passwords, DB URLs, JWT secrets, cookies, private keys, or tokens.

nodes.label: Single API Contract
nodes.rationale: Compare frontend API usage against backend implementation (route names, request body fields, response fields, auth requirement, role guards); on conflict keep the stricter security guard.

nodes.label: auth -> allowRoles -> scopeGuard Middleware Chain
nodes.rationale: Backend is the source of truth for permissions; frontend role guards are UX only and must mirror the backend.

nodes.label: Consistent JSON Response Shape
nodes.rationale: Return { success: true, data } or { success: false, message } for predictable frontend consumption.

nodes.label: MTMS Roles (super admin, admin, DIO, president, program director, secretary, supervisor, trainee)
nodes.rationale: Role names must be kept consistent across frontend, backend, and database.
nodes.norm_label: mtms roles (super admin, admin, dio, president, program director, secretary, supervisor, trainee)

nodes.label: Audit Logging (who, role, timestamp, action, record ID)
nodes.norm_label: audit logging (who, role, timestamp, action, record id)

nodes.label: bcrypt Password Hashing
nodes.rationale: Do not hardcode production passwords; use bcrypt or secure hashing.

nodes.label: Role-Based Dashboards
nodes.rationale: Keep admin, DIO, president, trainee, supervisor, program director, and secretary views separated; do not break role-based access.

nodes.label: Design Preservation
nodes.rationale: Do not change the design unless the user asks; preserve colors, spacing, logos, dashboard layout, and button/table/modal styles.

nodes.label: Defensive-Only Testing Rule
nodes.rationale: Only authorized defensive testing: no stealing secrets, no destructive attack steps, no real DoS, and never test servers not owned/authorized by the user.

nodes.label: Nginx Hidden File Blocking (deny .git)
nodes.rationale: Ensure Nginx blocks hidden files (location ~ /\. { deny all; }) so .git is not publicly served.

nodes.label: Test Credential Handling (prompt vs .env.test)
nodes.rationale: Allow credentials supplied directly in the prompt (used only for the current session, never saved/printed/committed) as Option 1, with local .env.test kept optional as Option 2; report only role and email.

nodes.label: Design System Generation
nodes.rationale: Always start with --design-system to search 5 domains in parallel and return a complete design system (pattern, style, colors, typography, effects, anti-patterns); optionally persist to MASTER.md plus page overrides.

nodes.label: Agent + Model Routing policy (split coding work by model)
nodes.norm_label: agent + model routing policy (split coding work by model)

nodes.label: Full-stack split-then-compare against single API contract
nodes.norm_label: full-stack split-then-compare against single api contract

nodes.norm_label: login/auth route testing flow (curl, token, refresh)

nodes.label: User Mongoose model (bcrypt cost 12, brute-force lockout)
nodes.norm_label: user mongoose model (bcrypt cost 12, brute-force lockout)

nodes.norm_label: 7-role rbac enum (super_admin, dio, president, program_director, secretary, supervisor, trainee)

nodes.label: Verify agent output over trusting its summary
nodes.norm_label: verify agent output over trusting its summary

nodes.norm_label: role_home + protectedroute redirect map (two copies must agree)

nodes.label: AuthContext session state + 14-min silent refresh timer
nodes.norm_label: authcontext session state + 14-min silent refresh timer

nodes.label: Golden rule for secrets (never print value, report path + rotate)
nodes.norm_label: golden rule for secrets (never print value, report path + rotate)

nodes.label: JWT handling (access JWT_SECRET 15m, refresh JWT_REFRESH_SECRET 7d httpOnly)
nodes.norm_label: jwt handling (access jwt_secret 15m, refresh jwt_refresh_secret 7d httponly)

nodes.norm_label: rbac + scope data isolation (allowroles + req.scope, prevent idor)

nodes.norm_label: login + role-redirect smoke test (role_home per role)

nodes.norm_label: npm verification scripts (check:backend, build:frontend, deploy:check, test:e2e:trainee)

nodes.label: UI/UX Pro Max design intelligence skill
nodes.norm_label: ui/ux pro max design intelligence skill

nodes.label: Accessibility rules (4.5:1 contrast, focus states, aria labels, touch targets)
nodes.norm_label: accessibility rules (4.5:1 contrast, focus states, aria labels, touch targets)

nodes.label: API Contract: Account Delete vs Deactivate
nodes.norm_label: api contract: account delete vs deactivate

nodes.label: Legacy roles removed (doctor/student/professor/director/admin)
nodes.rationale: Old roles removed from the active enum; run 'npm --prefix backend run migrate:legacy-roles' before deploying the tightened enum to a DB that may still hold old role values.

nodes.label: VPS Deployment (Nginx serves dist, PM2 mlms-backend)
nodes.rationale: frontend/dist is generated on the VPS from source during deploy and never committed; backend/uploads holds runtime data and must not be deleted during deployment.
nodes.norm_label: vps deployment (nginx serves dist, pm2 mlms-backend)

nodes.label: DELETE /api/admin/users/:id/permanent (Hard Delete)
nodes.rationale: The '/permanent' suffix is deliberate and must NEVER be a '?hard=true' flag on the soft DELETE. Only allowed when already Inactive (409 otherwise). Evaluations/Reports/Certificates/ConsultantMemos are preserved because they may legitimately reference a now-deleted user.

nodes.label: DOPS - Direct Observation of Procedural Skills Form
nodes.norm_label: dops - direct observation of procedural skills form

nodes.label: Internship FITER - Final In-Training Evaluation Report Form
nodes.norm_label: internship fiter - final in-training evaluation report form

nodes.label: Vision & Mission (National Center for Clinical Training)
nodes.norm_label: vision & mission (national center for clinical training)

nodes.label: Training Program Initiatives Feature
nodes.rationale: Each initiative is a proposal to create a new training specialty; it moves through a 3-stage approval pipeline shown as a Kanban board, living inside the existing /consultant-memo area.

nodes.label: ASG-only Access (INITIATIVE_ALLOWED_EMAILS allowlist)
nodes.rationale: Gating is account-level (email allowlist in env), not role-based, so ASG.3 can be added later with no code change. super_admin is deliberately NOT included. Backend returns 403 for non-allowlisted users; frontend guard is UX-only.

nodes.label: Initiative Data Model
nodes.rationale: Only checkpoint keys are stored (not labels) so the frontend can localize to AR/EN. Deletes are soft (deletedAt) to preserve history.

nodes.label: Initiatives API (auth -> requireInitiativeAccess)
nodes.rationale: Backend is the source of truth (403 on non-allowlisted); request bodies are field-whitelisted and never spread into Mongo filters to stay query-injection safe.
